events.title: Rat's ass with Tamsyn Kelly & Pernille Haaland
events.description: Brass Tacks Comedy in association with PARKAS at Independent Talent Tamsyn Kelly brings her critically acclaimed solo show Rat's Ass to SALT --- a sharp, personal and hilarious hour about class, compromise and money. After making more money than ever before last year, Tamsyn asks the uncomfortable question: what do you actually have to do to succeed? The answer might not be especially admirable. With razor-sharp observations and fearless honesty, she dives into the messy realities of social climbing, guilt and doing things you never thought you would --- all delivered with exceptional timing and undeniable stage presence. Tamsyn Kelly has appeared on HBO, ITV2, Comedy Central, BBC One, BBC Three and BBC Radio 4, and won the Edinburgh Comedy Awards Best in Class in 2022. Rolling Stone named Rat's Ass one of its "must-see comedy shows" of 2025. Joining her as a special guest is Norwegian-UK comedian Pernille Haaland. An award-winning stand-up comedian, actor and writer, Pernille is known from LATTER LIVE (NRK), Netflix's LOL, and BBC One's Not Going Out. She won Best Stand-Up Comedian at Reykjavík Fringe Festival 2024 and Best Joke at The Times Edinburgh Fringe Awards the same year. With sold-out tour dates across Europe and millions of views online, she has quickly become one of the most exciting Scandinavian comedians on the international circuit. An evening of uncompromising stand-up, uncomfortable truths and very, very good jokes.

events.title: Organ Recital with Johannes Zeinler
events.description: Johannes Zeinler (Vienna, Austria), organ Works by Muffat, Grigny, Martin, Mozart, Schönberg and Bach. Dear friends of organ music, Welcome to Oslo Cathedral and this year's summer concert series! Over the course of 15 Saturdays from July to October, we invite you to festive and diverse concerts featuring organists from both Norway and abroad. Each concert has its own distinct character, reflecting the musical background and tradition of the guest performer. During the summer months, when everyday life tends to slow down and time feels more spacious, we wish to present a rich and varied musical program on our cathedral organ, built in 1998 by the Norwegian organ builder Ryde & Berg. We are committed to making organ music accessible to as many people as possible. Please feel free to take the program with you, share it with friends and family, and invite someone along to a concert. Admission is always free for children and students. We look forward to seeing you at Oslo Cathedral and wish you a joyful and inspiring summer! Marcus André Berg and Tjark Pinne Organists of Oslo Cathedral. Accessibility: Oslo Cathedral has dedicated wheelchair spaces in the south transept. Companions enter free of charge upon presenting a companion certificate. Oslo Cathedral has a ramp and an elevator for step-free access. Please arrive at the main entrance, and our staff will guide you to the wheelchair entrance. A wheelchair-accessible restroom is available by the main entrance. For questions; please call Oslo Cathedral, phone: +47 23629010

events.title: Pet Shop Boys
events.description: Ticket Information: - Maximum 6 tickets per person. - Bottles, cans, and laser pointers are prohibited inside the concert area. Tickets will be available as mobile tickets (QR codes) via the Ticketmaster app and your user account well in advance of the concert (about 30 days). Learn more about delayed delivery of admission passes here. Tickets for Wheel chairs and companions Use public transportation to Unity Arena. Visit unityarena.no or ruter.no for travel options. For more details about the concert, artists, and entry times, visit unityarena.no or livenation.no. Schedule: - Box Office opens: TBA - Doors open: 18:00 - Concert starts: TBA General Information: The organizer reserves the right to deny entry to individuals who refuse personal inspection (this may include a body search and baggage check). See unityarena.no for details about prohibited items. Ejected individuals are not entitled to a ticket refund. There is no minimum age for the concert, but children under 15 must be accompanied by an adult (as per the Police Act in Bærum Municipality).

events.title: Paczes & Lotek 2
events.description: Paczes & Lotek live in Norway for the first time ever! On August 21, 2026, this iconic comedy duo will perform in Norway for the very first time. The artists will take the stage at Sentrum Scene, located in the heart of Oslo, as part of their 'Paczes & Lotek 2' tour. This duo needs no introduction - their shows have been selling out the biggest venues in Poland and abroad for years. Tickets are available now, and this is a truly unique opportunity, as another tour like this may not happen again for a long time! Date: August 21, 2026 (Friday) Venue: Sentrum Scene, Oslo Doors open: 4:30 PM Show starts: 5:15 PM Rules & Regulations: - Audio and video recording of the performance is strictly prohibited. - The ticket does not grant the right to disrupt the performers or the event. - The organizer reserves the right to remove any disruptive person without a ticket refund. - By purchasing a ticket, you agree to the rules stated above. There are unnumbered seats available at the event venue, available on a first-come, first-served basis. The event has an 18-year age limit with ID requirement. No guardian arrangement. Identification will be checked for all attendees assumed to be under 25. Valid identification includes a physical national ID card, passport, physical driver's license, or a photo ID bank card. Digital identification or photo of identification is not valid identification. The photo on the ID must be easily identifiable as the user's. Read the organizer's terms of purchase here. See www.rockefeller.no for the complete program.. Practical information about the concerts can be found here. The nearest transport hub is Storgata and/or Jernbanetorget. See ruter.no for more info.
